doc.trigger: Receives a webhook trigger when a Fireflies transcript is ready for analysis.
doc.overview: This workflow analyzes sales call transcripts from Fireflies using OpenAI to extract PID (Pain, Impact, Decision) elements, score them, and generate coaching reports. Results and coaching materials are stored and emailed to reps, with records managed via Supabase integration. The workflow ensures reps get actionable feedback for continuous improvement while centralizing records for review and analytics.
phases: Transcript Capture
Receives and parses call transcripts from Fireflies.
PID Analysis & Scoring
Uses OpenAI to extract, analyze, and score key sales call elements (Pain, Impact, Decision).
Reporting & Coaching
Generates and sends coaching reports via email and updates Supabase with analytics and transcript summaries.
nodes: Fetches the sales call transcript from Fireflies.
Cleans transcript text and maps to the correct rep record.
Extracts key PID elements from the transcript.
Parses and structures the extracted PID summary.
Analyzes the transcript for additional call insights.
Processes and structures the extracted call intelligence.
Consolidates PID and call intelligence outputs.
Compiles data into a single call analysis record.
Scores the 'Pain' element in the transcript.
Scores the 'Impact' element in the transcript.
Scores the 'Decision' element in the transcript.
Combines all PID scores into a unified data structure.
Finalizes scoring and readiness for reporting.
Sends a detailed analytical report to relevant stakeholders.
Delivers targeted coaching suggestions to the sales rep.
Determines if the call is internal and should be skipped.
Queries Supabase to retrieve rep records.
Associates transcript data with corresponding video call details.
Upserts or updates the call analysis record in Supabase.

Saves the full call transcript in Supabase.

Checks if the transcript exists before proceeding further.
Syncs deal information or tags with an external system.
doc.notes: Monitor OpenAI API quotas and Fireflies webhook reliability; validate Supabase and email integrations after schema or credential changes.

doc.trigger: Starts when a new lead arrives via a webhook.
doc.overview: This workflow automates the qualification of inbound leads by verifying their contact information, enriching their profiles, and syncing results to both Supabase and Pipedrive. It streamlines the decision-making process, leveraging data verification APIs and AI to ensure high-quality leads are nurtured or rejected efficiently. Qualified leads are pushed to CRM systems, while unqualified ones are tracked in Supabase for reporting.
phases: Data Ingestion
Collect lead data from the inbound webhook and restore the incoming payload.
Lead Enrichment & Verification
Verify and enrich contact details using third-party services, and run AI-based validation checks.
Qualification Logic
Merge enrichment results, apply qualification rules, and decide on next steps for each lead.
CRM & Database Sync
Sync qualified leads and associated objects to Pipedrive, update Supabase with deal and lead statuses.
nodes: Receives inbound lead data to trigger the workflow.
Reconstructs the webhook payload for downstream processing.
Builds rejection information for leads that do not qualify.

doc.notes: Maintain and periodically monitor third-party API credentials, as changes or downtime in external validation services may impact workflow reliability.

doc.trigger: Triggered when a deal is created, updated, or deleted in Pipedrive.
doc.overview: This workflow synchronizes deal data between Pipedrive and Supabase, while also calculating and storing the time a deal spends in each sales pipeline stage. The process ensures data consistency across systems and provides visibility on deal progression timeframes.
phases: Deal Event Detection
Detects changes to deals in Pipedrive, including updates and deletions.
Stage Time Calculation & Sync
Calculates time spent in each pipeline stage and updates this data in both Pipedrive and Supabase.

Checks if the deal belongs to the 'SALES 2026' pipeline for conditional processing.

doc.notes: Ensure API rate limits for both Pipedrive and Supabase are monitored, and update field mappings promptly if pipeline stages or custom fields change.
doc._name: Sync CRM with Supabase + Add Time per Stage

doc.trigger: A scheduled trigger starts the workflow daily.
doc.overview: This workflow automates the batch generation of transcript embeddings using OpenAI, ensuring all new transcript data in Supabase is regularly processed for downstream ML applications. It operates on a daily schedule for up-to-date embeddings.
phases: Fetch Unembedded Transcripts
Retrieves transcripts from Supabase that have not yet been embedded.
Generate and Store Embeddings
Processes retrieved transcripts with OpenAI to generate and update vector embeddings in Supabase.
nodes: Initiates the workflow daily based on a timed schedule.
Fetches all transcripts from Supabase missing embeddings.
Formats and prepares transcript data for embedding generation.
Sends batch transcript data to OpenAI API to create embeddings.
Processes embedding results and prepares them for database update.
Updates Supabase records with the new embeddings.
doc.notes: Ensure OpenAI and Supabase API credentials are valid and monitor for API rate limits or failures from either service.

doc.trigger: Triggered by an incoming webhook when a new lead is submitted from an event or course.
doc.overview: This workflow captures leads generated from events or courses and automates their entry into the Pipedrive CRM. It processes the incoming lead data, organizes it, and creates associated records for deals, people, and organizations in Pipedrive. This ensures that event/course leads are systematically tracked and managed for sales follow-up.
phases: Intake
The workflow receives and processes incoming lead data from events or courses.
CRM Entry
Lead information is standardized and used to create or update corresponding records in Pipedrive.
nodes: Accepts incoming lead data from an external source.
Processes and formats lead data for CRM compatibility.
Applies custom logic to manipulate specific lead properties.
Combines multiple data streams before CRM creation steps.
Creates a new organization entry in Pipedrive if needed.
Creates or updates a person contact in Pipedrive.
Creates a new deal in Pipedrive linked to the lead.
Performs final processing or cleanup operations on the lead data.
doc.notes: Ensure Pipedrive API credentials are current and test webhook connectivity after any schema changes.

doc.trigger: Triggered by a booked Discovery call event from Calendly.
doc.overview: This workflow automates the process of creating or updating a Pipedrive deal when a Discovery call is booked via Calendly. It validates contact data, checks for existing records, syncs updates to Supabase, and enriches contact and deal information. This ensures CRM hygiene and reduces manual work in sales pipeline management.
phases: Event Detection
Listens for newly booked Discovery calls via a Calendly trigger.
Person & Deal Matching
Checks and creates Pipedrive person and deal records, including data validation and deduplication.
Data Enrichment
Validates and enriches email, phone, and job data using third-party services and AI.
Deal Sync & Update
Synchronizes and updates deal data between Pipedrive and Supabase, including ownership and activity notes.

doc._name: Create/Update a deal from Discovery Call (Supabase)

doc.trigger: Workflow starts when any error is detected via an error trigger.
doc.overview: This workflow monitors for errors in n8n executions, automatically analyzes and attempts auto-fixes for retryable errors, and notifies relevant team members by email. It ensures reliable operations by providing prompt visibility and preventing repeated issues from going unaddressed.
phases: Error Detection
Initiates when an error occurs and filters out irrelevant workflows.
Automated Remediation & Alerting
Attempts automated fixes for retryable errors and informs stakeholders via tailored email alerts.
nodes: Detects and starts the workflow on n8n execution errors.
Filters errors to process only those from specified workflows.
Implements throttling to avoid excessive processing or duplicate alerts.
Collects metadata necessary for subsequent analysis and decisions.
Calls an external service or API to analyze the nature of the detected error.
Processes the analysis result and determines the recommended action.
Checks if the error can be automatically retried.
Attempts to re-execute the failed workflow if it is retryable.
Evaluates the outcome of the retry attempt for success or failure.
Sends a notification email about the outcome of the automated retry.
Sends an alert email for non-retryable or unrecoverable errors.
doc.notes: Keep workflow credentials for HTTP and email nodes up to date; ensure rate limiting logic is effective to prevent notification spam.

doc.trigger: Runs every 23 hours using a schedule trigger.
doc.overview: This workflow automates the generation and update of documentation for all n8n workflows, committing changes directly to a GitHub repository. It detects updates, generates documentation using OpenAI, and ensures documentation stays synchronized with the latest workflow changes.
phases: Fetch & Split Workflows
Periodically requests all workflows, splits them out, and filters for active ones.
Summarize & Detect Changes
Summarizes workflow details, checks against previously cached data, and identifies new or updated workflows.
Generate & Merge Docs
Creates documentation using OpenAI, aggregates new docs, and merges them into final HTML.
Commit Updates
Builds the HTML file, updates the cache, fetches the target file SHA, and commits the new documentation to GitHub.
nodes: Triggers the workflow on a 23-hour schedule.
Fetches all available workflows from the n8n instance.
Splits the list of workflows for processing individually.
Filters out inactive workflows from the processing stream.
Retrieves detailed information for each active workflow.
Builds workflow summaries and hashes for change detection.
Groups all summaries for bulk comparison and aggregation.
Retrieves the previous cache to detect changes since last run.
Compares new and cached summaries to identify changed workflows.
Branches execution if there are detected changes.
Splits out only the workflows that have changed.
Uses OpenAI to generate natural language documentation for workflows.
Appends workflow names to the generated documentation.
Collects new documentation snippets for final assembly.
Merges all new and existing documentation together.
Constructs the final HTML documentation file.
Prepares the latest summaries for cache update.
Saves the updated workflow summaries cache.
Fetches the GitHub file SHA for the documentation file.
Commits the updated documentation HTML to the GitHub repository.
doc.notes: Keep the OpenAI API credentials secured and be mindful of rate limits from both GitHub and n8n endpoints; verify the cache file integrity regularly for consistent change detection.
doc._name: Live update on Github documentations for N8N workflows

doc.trigger: Triggered by receiving a phone call via webhook.
doc.overview: This workflow processes incoming Ringostat phone call data, transcribes call audio, analyzes key conversational elements using OpenAI, and synchronizes results with Supabase. It enables sales teams to automatically evaluate and score call quality, extract actionable insights, and store transcripts for further use. The workflow automates call intelligence, boosting sales operations efficiency.
phases: Call Reception and Preprocessing
The workflow receives incoming call data and prepares it for analysis.
Audio Processing and Analysis
Downloads and transcribes call audio, then analyzes transcript for pain points, intelligence, and sales quality scores using AI.
Data Mapping and Storage
Maps analysis results and upserts them, along with transcripts, to Supabase and synchronizes related deal tags.

Synchronizes deal tags based on the short call analysis.
doc.notes: Review all external API keys and Supabase table structures regularly, as schema changes or expired credentials can break upsertion or analysis.

doc.trigger: Triggered by an incoming HTTP webhook containing phone call data.
doc.overview: This workflow analyzes phone call recordings by transcribing, extracting key information, evaluating performance, and syncing results to Supabase. It streamlines call intelligence processes for sales teams, automating pain point extraction and score calculations to improve deal handling and rep performance. The workflow ensures structured storage and integration for further analytics and reporting.
phases: Input & Preprocessing
Receives call input, cleans up data, and downloads necessary audio files.
Transcription & Analysis
Transcribes the audio, runs OpenAI models to extract insights and scores, then merges and organizes results.
Database Sync & Deal Management
Maps, prepares, and upserts activities, analyses, transcripts, and deal tags in Supabase.

doc.notes: Ensure Supabase API endpoints and credentials are kept up to date; regularly test OpenAI node performance for prompt accuracy.

doc.trigger: Fires when the webhook endpoint receives a request.
doc.overview: This workflow receives enriched deal data from Clay via webhook and updates the corresponding deal in Pipedrive. It ensures CRM records are continuously enhanced with the latest external data, streamlining sales operations.
phases: Receive Enrichment Data
Collects new or updated deal information from Clay through a webhook.
Update Pipedrive
Pushes the enriched information into the relevant Pipedrive deal.
nodes: Receives incoming HTTP requests containing enriched deal data from Clay.
Updates the corresponding Pipedrive deal with the new enrichment data.
doc.notes: Ensure the webhook endpoint is securely managed and the data mapping aligns with Pipedrive field requirements.
